tldr: Primer-to-PortOne only makes sense if your business is Korea-heavy; you trade Primer's broad global orchestration and workflow tooling for PortOne's deeper Korean PG ecosystem.
why_switch: PortOne has dominant coverage of Korean PGs (KakaoPay, Toss, NaverPay, KG Inicis, etc.)
Localized Korean checkout UX is stronger out of the box than rebuilding it on Primer
If most of your traffic is Korean, paying for Primer's global orchestration breadth is overkill
PortOne's usage-based pricing model can be a cleaner fit than Primer's hybrid enterprise contracts
Single SDK across 20+ Korean and global PGs reduces integration burden in-region
what_to_export: List of connected PSPs and routing rules in Primer
Customer profiles and saved payment methods
export_method: Use Primer's official APIs and dashboard to export transactions, customers, payment methods, and any workflow definitions. Primer's orchestration workflows are platform-specific and will not import into PortOne — capture them as documentation for re-implementation. Verify exact endpoints and export formats in Primer's official docs.
import_method: Set up a PortOne merchant account, configure the Korean and global PGs you need, and rebuild checkout against PortOne's SDK. Customer and token data is generally PSP-scoped, so coordinate token portability with each underlying PG rather than expecting orchestrator-to-orchestrator transfer. Refer to PortOne's official onboarding documentation for current connector list and SDK setup.

concept_mapping.note: Re-implement orchestration logic using PortOne's routing/SDK or in your own backend
Connector catalogs differ; confirm each PG's availability
Re-build checkout UI; configurations are not portable
Different event schema; re-subscribe and re-map handlers
Field mapping per PortOne docs; tokens typically not portable

gotchas: Primer's Workflows have no direct PortOne analog — plan to rebuild that logic
PortOne's strength is Korean coverage; non-Korean PG depth may be thinner
PortOne's docs are strongest in Korean — ensure team reference is available
Stored payment tokens are typically PSP-scoped; coordinate with each PG for portability
Webhook schemas and reconciliation outputs differ — finance and ops need lead time
Run parallel for at least one full reconciliation cycle before decommissioning Primer

email.subject: Migration plan: Primer to PortOne
email.body:
Team,

Proposing we move payments orchestration from Primer to PortOne. This only makes sense if our traffic is Korea-heavy — PortOne's strength is its 20+ Korean PG coverage, while Primer is broader globally and has stronger workflow tooling.

Plan:
1. Confirm Korean traffic share justifies the swap; otherwise hold.
2. Inventory Primer Workflows, PSPs, and webhooks; document logic that will need re-implementation.
3. Stand up PortOne, configure required Korean and global PGs, rebuild checkout on the PortOne SDK.
4. Run both stacks in parallel; cut over progressively.
5. Decommission Primer after a clean reconciliation cycle.

Estimated effort: 3-8 weeks. Specifics will be verified against Primer and PortOne official docs.

Thanks
